content.problemStatement: Given an m x n grid of characters board and a string word, return true if the word exists in the grid. The word can be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells, where adjacent cells are horizontally or vertically neighboring. The same letter cell may not be used more than once.
constraints: board[i][j] is a lowercase English letter
word consists of lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The input is a 2D grid of characters (board) and a string (word). The board is represented as a list of lists where each inner list represents a row in the grid. The word is a string of characters.
content.outputFormat: The output is a boolean value indicating whether the word exists in the grid.

sampleTestCases.explanation: We can easily find the given word in the matrix.
There is no such word in the given matrix.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use depth-first search to explore all possible paths in the grid and check if the word can be formed.
